content.claim: Free school choice is a statutory right guaranteed under the Act on Public Education (2011. évi CXC. törvény a nemzeti köznevelésről) and has been explicitly stated as non-negotiable by the current education minister.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Does not quantify the extent to which free choice is exercised in practice
Does not measure interaction effects between free choice and early selection in creating segregation
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
